title: Computer Networks
description: Covers network structure, packet routing, packet delays, and the IP suite layers.

questions.prompt: What is the name for the delay caused by pushing a packet of length L onto a link of bandwidth R (that is, L/R)?
Match each source of packet delay to its description.
right: Time to check bit errors and determine the output link
Time a packet waits at the output link (depends on congestion)
Distance divided by the signal speed along the link
Time to push the packet bits onto the link (L/R)

questions.prompt: Which of the following are advantages of packet switching over circuit switching?
options: Different flows can share links along their route
Good resource utilisation under bursty traffic
Flows can reroute around failed or congested links

questions.prompt: Which link-layer protocol resolves an IP address to a MAC address for a device on the same subnet?
Which of the following are TRUE about layering in the network stack?
options: Layer N uses services of layer N-1 and provides services to layer N+1
It is easy to swap out a layer's implementation
Every layer adds a header to the data unit it passes on

questions.prompt: Match each IP suite layer number to its name.
What is the term for the rate (in bits per second) at which bits are successfully transferred in a given flow?
Which of the following are TRUE about the store-and-forward principle?
options: A router must receive the entire packet before it can start forwarding
Each hop adds a transmission delay of L/R for the packet
Packets are forwarded bit-by-bit as they arrive
The bottleneck link determines steady-state throughput for a flow
It is the reason the first packet suffers more transmission delay than later ones in a pipelined transfer
